name: Stack Layout Intent
summary: A primitive layout region-role (#1680 taxonomy): composition-intent = even-spaced flow of siblings along the block axis. Identity is the arrangement, not the CSS — FUI ships one block per role and the mechanism (margin-flow or flex-column + gap) is an impl seam. Project-less; per-project styling rides presentational variants + plateau assembler presets.

gap.description: The consistent spacing inserted between stacked siblings, on a spacing scale — the role's one essential knob. Optional with a conventional default; open-numbered per the variant contract.

recursion.description: Whether the consistent spacing applies to every nested descendant level (a recursive stack) or only the direct children (shallow). Default shallow.

split.description: An optional split point — a child index after which the remaining children are pushed to the far end (via auto-margin), so a header/footer pins while the middle flows. Absent → no split.

description:
What This Is

The most fundamental layout role: a one-dimensional, even-spaced flow of stacked siblings — consistent spacing between items and none at the ends. The block-axis workhorse of a page: form fields, article blocks, the contents of a card.

Composition-intent (the identity)

A primitive layout region-role (layoutTier: region-role), one of the per-role taxonomy ratified in #1680. Its identity is the composition-intent — even-spaced flow of siblings along the block axis — impl-agnostic. FUI ships exactly one block for this role; the CSS mechanism (margin-flow (> * + *) or flex-column + gap) is an impl seam, never the role's identity (platform-decisions.md#layout-role-composition-intent).

Dimensions (UX-only)

  • gap: The consistent spacing inserted between stacked siblings, on a spacing scale — the role's one essential knob. Optional with a conventional default; open-numbered per the variant contract (none · xs · sm · md · lg · xl).
  • recursion: Whether the consistent spacing applies to every nested descendant level (a recursive stack) or only the direct children (shallow). Default shallow (shallow · recursive).
  • split: An optional split point — a child index after which the remaining children are pushed to the far end (via auto-margin), so a header/footer pins while the middle flows. Absent → no split.
